extra.message: User input from params['id'] is directly concatenated into SQL SELECT statement without sanitization or parameterized queries. This allows SQL injection attacks.

User input from params['path'] is passed to open() after os.path.abspath() normalization, but there is no whitelist or restriction on which files can be accessed. An attacker can read arbitrary files on the system using path traversal sequences.

cwe: CWE-22: Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory

extra.message: User input from params['domain'] is directly concatenated into shell command 'nslookup' which is executed with shell=True. This allows arbitrary OS command injection.

cwe: CWE-78: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an OS Command

extra.message: User input from params['comment'] and time.ctime() are directly interpolated into SQL INSERT statement using string formatting without parameterized queries. This allows SQL injection attacks.

User input from params['include'] is used to load and execute arbitrary Python code via exec(). The file can be loaded from local filesystem or remote URL via urllib.request.urlopen(), allowing remote code execution.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')
CWE-95: Improper Neutralization of Directives in Dynamically Evaluated Code ('Eval Injection')

extra.message: User input from params.get('password', '') is directly concatenated into SQL WHERE clause without sanitization. Although the username is sanitized with re.sub(r'[^\w]', ''), the password field remains vulnerable to SQL injection, allowing authentication bypass.
User input from params['object'] is decoded and passed directly to pickle.loads() without validation. Pickle deserialization of untrusted data allows arbitrary code execution.
User input from params['name'] is directly interpolated into XPath expression without sanitization. This allows XPath injection attacks to manipulate the query and extract unauthorized data.

cwe: CWE-643: Improper Neutralization of Data within XPath Expressions

extra.message: XML parsing is configured with load_dtd=True, resolve_entities=True, and no_network=False, which enables XXE (XML External Entity) attacks. User input from params['xml'] is passed to the parser.

cwe: CWE-611: Improper Restriction of XML External Entity Reference

extra.message: User input from params['redir'] is directly inserted into an HTTP meta refresh tag without validation. This allows open redirect attacks to redirect users to arbitrary malicious websites.

cwe: CWE-601: URL Redirection to Untrusted Site

extra.message: User input from params['callback'] is directly used as JSONP callback function name without validation. This allows JSONP injection attacks where arbitrary function names can be injected.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation

extra.message: User input from params['v'] is directly substituted into HTML content via regex replacement without encoding. This allows reflected XSS attacks.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')
